mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in retail sales indicates a demand-supply mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60d returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 320, an increase in the federal funds rate accelerates real borrowing costs, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly negative when both FEDFUNDS rises and CPIAUCSL exceeds the 320 threshold, or the signal loses predictive power in a low-inflation regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in housing starts reflects rising consumer confidence and construction activity, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to produce positive 5d returns in > 50% of monthly observations over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds 250000 in a single month, it signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, ultimately weighing on equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly negative following months where PAYEMS exceeds 250,000, or the signal flips to positive during historical overheating episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with declining real retail sales indicates a structural demand collapse that forces aggressive corporate deleveraging, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint rise in UNRATE and fall in RSAFS does not predict negative 60d returns in more than 50% of historical recession onset periods, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets where the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, a decline in industrial production signals supply chain bottlenecks that disrupt corporate input costs, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 20d returns when UNRATE is below 4.0%, or the signal's predictive power disappears in high-unemployment periods.
